item.title: Cardiff set to become the home of Fijian Rugby on 4 July
item.description: Cardiff is set to become the home of Fijian rugby for one special day when the FIJI Water Flying Fijians play Wales, with Fiji as the home team. This was shared by the Fiji Rugby Union.
item.content:encoded:
Cardiff is set to become the home of Fijian rugby for one special day when the FIJI Water Flying Fijians play Wales, with Fiji as the home team.

This was shared by the Fiji Rugby Union.

FRU says that, as a Fiji home match, the city will come alive with the sights, sounds, and spirit of the Pacific Islands, from traditional warriors and cultural performers welcoming supporters on their journey to the stadium, to a vibrant Fan Zone filled with live music, authentic Fijian experiences, and family entertainment.

This promises to be unlike any other match day.

FRU says that as supporters make their way to the stadium, they will be greeted by Fijian warriors in traditional attire, creating a unique and memorable atmosphere.

Traditional Fijian dancers will also perform along the route, offering a warm cultural welcome and setting the stage for a true Fijian summer celebration.

They say that, located just outside the stadium, the official Fan Zone will allow supporters to immerse themselves in authentic Fijian culture before kick-off.

At the heart of the Fan Zone will be a traditional Fijian village featuring authentic bures, the iconic thatched houses found throughout the islands.

FRU adds that fans can enjoy a range of cultural activities, including receiving temporary tribal tattoos from specialist artists, creating their own Fijian-inspired tote bags, dressing in authentic Fijian costumes, and learning more about Fiji’s islands, traditions, and cultural heritage.

They say the entertainment will continue throughout the day with performances from some of Fiji’s most celebrated acts, including Paradise Rootz, Primanavia—Fiji’s award-winning choir that blends classical choral excellence with rich Pacific traditions—and Georgina Ledua, one of Fiji’s mos t beloved singers and songwriters.

They will be joined by DJ Kingsman, the UK’s leading Fijian DJ, who will keep the energy and excitement building throughout the event.

They say supporters are encouraged to embrace the festive spirit by wearing colourful Bula shirts, Hawaiian shirts, grass skirts, and other Pacific-inspired outfits.

FRU says the best-dressed fans will have the opportunity to win exciting prizes, helping transform Cardiff into a vibrant sea of colour and the ultimate summer rugby celebration.

Fiji will play Wales next Saturday at Cardiff City Stadium at 1.10am.

item.title: Labasa and Savusavu legends to clash before Fiji FACT final
item.description: Former football stars from Labasa and Savusavu will reignite one of the North's traditional rivalries when they meet in a Legends match ahead of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT Grand Final at Subrail Park on Sunday. The Fiji Football Association says the match will feature a number of former district representatives from both sides and has been included to celebrate the contribution of past players to foot
item.content:encoded:
Former football stars from Labasa and Savusavu will reignite one of the North's traditional rivalries when they meet in a Legends match ahead of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT Grand Final at Subrail Park on Sunday.

The Fiji Football Association says the match will feature a number of former district representatives from both sides and has been included to celebrate the contribution of past players to football in the Northern Division.

The curtain-raiser will kick off at 1pm, giving fans the opportunity to watch some of the North's former greats back in action before the Fiji FACT Grand Final begins at 3pm.

Meanwhile, Ba will face Labasa in the first semi-final at 2pm tomorrow, while Navua will take on Lautoka in the second semi-final at 4.30pm.

item.title: Home semi-final spot driving Drua Women ahead of crucial Reds clash
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's Head Coach Mike Legge says they have made only one positional change to the side ahead of their cruci

item.title: Faf de Klerk to captain Babas, while Vakatawa moves to wing

item.title: Consistency key as Drua Women chase semi-final spot
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's Head Coach Mike Legge says while the team's ultimate aspiration is to win the Super Rugby Women's com

item.title: Brazil finish strongly with 3-0 win over Scotland in Group C clash
item.description: Vinícius Júnior scored twice as Brazil sealed a comfortable 3-0 victory over Scotland in their final Group C match at the FIFA World Cup. Brazil took a 2-0 lead into half-time after goals from Vinícius and Matheus Cunha before Vinícius added his second in the second half to complete the victory.
item.content:encoded:
Vinícius Júnior scored twice as Brazil sealed a comfortable 3-0 victory over Scotland in their final Group C match at the FIFA World Cup.

Brazil took a 2-0 lead into half-time after goals from Vinícius and Matheus Cunha before Vinícius added his second in the second half to complete the victory.

Meanwhile, Switzerland defeated Canada 2-1 in Group B, while Morocco beat Haiti 4-2 in the other final Group C match.

Czechia will face Mexico, while South Africa meets South Korea at 1pm.

Coming up tomorrow, Curaçao takes on Côte d'Ivoire and Ecuador faces Germany at 8am. At 11am, Tunisia meets the Netherlands while Japan takes on Sweden. At 2pm, co-hosts the United States face Türkiye, while Australia takes on Paraguay.

item.title: Switzerland beat Canada 2-1 to top Group B in FIFA clash
item.description: Switzerland beat co-hosts Canada 2-1 to finish top of Group B and qualify for the FIFA World Cup knockout stage. The teams went into half-time level at 0-0.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland beat co-hosts Canada 2-1 to finish top of Group B and qualify for the FIFA World Cup knockout stage.

The teams went into half-time level at 0-0.

Canada also advanced despite the defeat, reaching the knockout stage for the first time in their history after finishing second in the group.

Bosnia and Herzegovina defeated Qatar 3-1 in their final Group B match at the FIFA World Cup.

Brazil are currently taking on Scotland while Morocco is currently playing Haiti.

Czechia will play Mexico and South Africa meet South Korea at 1pm.

item.title: Ba Women aim to shine in OFC Women's Champions League
item.description: Ba Women will have the opportunity to test themselves against some of Oceania's top clubs when they compete in the OFC Women's Champions League in Honiara, Solomon Islands this weekend. Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says the team is well prepared and ready for the challenge ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Ba Women will have the opportunity to test themselves against some of Oceania's top clubs when they compete in the OFC Women's Champions League in Honiara, Solomon Islands this weekend.

Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says the team is well prepared and ready for the challenge ahead.

Dayal has urged the players to represent both Ba and Fiji with pride and determination throughout the tournament.

He says the competition is a special opportunity for the players to showcase their talent against some of the best clubs in Oceania.

Dayal says he has full confidence in the team and has encouraged the players to give their all in every match, stay united and make their supporters and the nation proud.

Ba will begin its Group B campaign against hosts Henderson Eels FC on Sunday at 5pm before taking on Papua New Guinea champions Hekari Women FC next Wednesday.

The Women in Black will then face Vanuatu's Tafea FC next Saturday in their final pool match.

The top two teams from each group will advance to the semi-finals, with the final scheduled for July 10th.

item.description: The Northland Rugby Union has received a significant boost ahead of its Fiji Rugby Union Vodafone Vanua Cup elimination campaign f
